source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / A/B evaluation protocol
description: Before any AROMER world model update is committed, it must pass an A/B gate: the updated model must outperform the baseline on a held-out episode set.

required_improvement.ece: B <= A + 0.02 (calibration must not worsen significantly)

protocol.hard_requirement: B must not regress on critical_tier accuracy or safety invariant recall

payload.aromer_constraint: World model not committed until A/B gate passed. Shadow mode used during test period.

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / logical consistency check
description: The world model must not contain internal contradictions: a domain cannot have both high p_harm and high acceptance rate. Contradictions are resolved before committing updates.

contradiction_types: p_harm > 0.7 in domain X AND accept_rate > 0.5 in domain X → contradiction
safety_invariant says escalate AND world_model says accept for same key → contradiction
High confidence claim AND 0 supporting episodes → suspicious
resolution_protocol: 1. Identify contradiction source (seed vs learned vs oracle conflict)
2. Rank by evidence weight (artifact > human_verified > multi_oracle)
4. If unresolvable → flag for human review, keep conservative estimate
payload.aromer_constraint: contradiction_debt metric tracked. If contradiction_debt > threshold → pause new updates

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / graduated autonomy
description: AROMER autonomy levels are earned through demonstrated performance, not assumed. Each autonomy level requires passing specific performance thresholds.

level_0_supervised.description: All decisions logged and available for human review

level_1_advisory.description: AROMER recommendations shown to operator but operator decides

level_2_gated_autonomy.description: AROMER decides low/medium risk. High/critical still human-gated.
level_2_gated_autonomy.threshold: >=500 episodes, false_accept_rate < 0.05, ECE < 0.10, A/B gate passed

level_3_full_governance.description: AROMER governs all tiers. Humans review escalations only.
level_3_full_governance.threshold: >=2000 episodes, false_accept_rate < 0.02, safety invariant recall = 1.0, external validation

payload.aromer_rule: AROMER starts at level_0. Autonomy upgrades require explicit operator approval after gate passage.
payload.demotion: Any regression on critical safety metrics immediately demotes to level_0

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / regression prevention
description: Every update to AROMER's world model must pass a regression gate: no previously passing metric is allowed to degrade beyond tolerance.

payload.regression_response: Hard block on world model commit. Log regression report artifact. Require human review.
payload.aromer_constraint: Regression gate runs automatically after every world model update cycle

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / safety metric gate
description: Safety metrics are hard gates — not soft targets. If any safety metric fails, the update is blocked unconditionally, regardless of improvements elsewhere.

payload.failure_response: Immediate rollback to last safe world model state. Alert operator. Log to audit trail.
payload.aromer_constraint: Safety gate failure is the only condition that automatically triggers human escalation regardless of AROMER autonomy level
payload.principle: Seed packs skal ikke gjøre AROMER skråsikker. Safety gates are the enforcement mechanism for this principle.
